tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and mild ketonuria, with no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another form of chronic kidney disease. Further nephrological evaluation and assessment of glycemic control are recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
